meta.sector: fintech / quantitative trading intelligence (India NSE F&O)

problem.statement: Day-trading NIFTY 50 and BANKNIFTY F&O requires synthesizing multi-timeframe candlestick behavior, open interest, India VIX regime, heavyweight-constituent and sector momentum, support/resistance confluence, and intraday seasonality faster than a human can do manually and consistently. The founder has a proven manual mental model (candle-negation cascade across timeframes, cross-checked against NIFTY/BNF heavyweights) but is time-boxed and cannot watch every timeframe/constituent/window simultaneously, and has no systematic way to log outcomes and improve the model over time.
target_users: The founder (primary user): active NIFTY/BNF F&O day trader executing manually via Zerodha
Small circle of trusted co-traders (secondary, v1.1+) who trade the same instruments
jobs_to_be_done: See, at a glance, the highest-conviction tactical trade opportunities for the next 15m/30m/1h/2h with a confidence score and a data-backed rationale
Understand *why* a recommendation was made (candle pattern, negation prediction, heavyweight/sector confirmation, OI, VIX regime) via a collapsible reasoning tree, not a black box
See a 2-5 day strategic outlook (accumulation zones, breakout probability) separate from tactical day-trade calls
Get a flagged, lower-conviction warning on expiry days, plus a same-window BTST suggestion when the pattern supports it
Get notified (Telegram + dashboard) the moment a high-conviction setup forms, without needing to watch every chart
Log the outcome of a trade (win/loss/observation) so the system's pattern and negation-timing weights improve over time
Practice new ideas risk-free in a paper-trading mode before trusting them live
Feed in a strategy observed elsewhere (a YouTube video, a rule set, a Pine Script) and get it independently backtested and scored before trusting it
Configure their own strike-selection rules (RSI/candle-type/OI thresholds) rather than being locked into a fixed system heuristic

core_features_mvp: Multi-timeframe candlestick pattern detection (Engulfing, 3-candle inside/outside, Harami, Doji, Pin Bar) across 5m/10m/15m/30m/1h/2h/3h for NIFTY, BANKNIFTY, and a configurable heavyweight/sector watchlist

Candle-negation cascade model: predicts the window in which a detected pattern is likely to be negated, scaled by the pattern's formation timeframe and current India VIX regime (heuristic MVP, LSTM upgrade path defined)
Multi-timeframe support/resistance engine (Weekly to Daily to 4h/1h/30m/5m) using pivot levels + historical hit-frequency confluence
Heavyweight & sector correlation engine: scores whether top-15-by-weight NIFTY constituents and sector indices (BANK/IT/FMCG/PHARMA/AUTO) confirm the index-level pattern

Intraday seasonality + impulse-move detection: dedicated scan at 10:00, 11:00, 12:30 (EU-open proxy), 13:30, 14:00, and 14:30-15:00 IST for institutional-flow-style impulse moves, tagged as a distinct recommendation category

Multi-factor confidence scoring: Confidence = 0.25*macro_SR_alignment + 0.25*heavyweight_pattern_alignment + 0.20*strike_candle_pattern + 0.15*OI_accumulation + 0.15*RSI_alignment, plus a separate risk score (VIX regime, expiry proximity, liquidity) and a conviction score

Four recommendation categories: (A) Tactical day-trade (15m/30m/1h/2h, entry/exit/strike), (B) Impulse/institutional-flow moves, (C) 2-5 day strategic outlook (direction, accumulation zones), (D) Expiry-day BTST

Collapsible AI reasoning tree per recommendation: shows which layer (macro bias, pattern, negation prediction, heavyweight confirmation, OI, seasonality) contributed and how much, backed by the actual data points used
User-configurable strike-selection rules (RSI thresholds, candle-type filters, OI-accumulation thresholds, IV rank) that override the system's default heuristic per-user
Feedback loop: manual trade-outcome logging (win/loss/observation) feeding a Bayesian pattern/negation-weight update job (online-learning upgrade path defined for v1.1+)
Paper-trading simulator: replays live or historical data against open recommendations to produce simulated fills and P&L, with an explicit switch between paper and live-manual modes

Strategy Marketplace: ingest a strategy as a YouTube URL, free-text description, pseudocode, or Pine Script; extract canonical logic; run an independent backtest against historical TimescaleDB data; assign a confidence score; export as Pine Script or a Python backtest function
Strategy fusion: combine the founder's own rules with a marketplace strategy and backtest the merged rule set independently before it's trusted
Telegram bot + dashboard alerts for newly fired recommendations; email as an optional third channel
Risk guardrails: India VIX regime thresholds that suppress or downgrade tactical recommendations, expiry-day conviction dampening, no-trade flags for low-liquidity strikes
features_v2: Trained LSTM negation-timing model replacing the MVP heuristic, once sufficient labeled trade-journal data exists
Online/incremental learning for pattern and negation weights (replacing manual-approval batch Bayesian updates)
Automated video transcription + strategy extraction pipeline running unattended (MVP requires a manual trigger per submission)
Multi-user / small-team access with per-user watchlists and shared strategy marketplace
Broader constituent coverage (all 50 NIFTY stocks in real time, not just top 15 by weight)
Additional alternative data feeds (options-flow vendors, NSE max-pain data) if a reliable, ToS-compliant source is identified
explicit_non_goals: NEVER places, modifies, or cancels a real order — no automated or 'live-algo' execution path exists in this codebase, now or as a flag; recommendations are read/notify only
NEVER touches real money or a funded brokerage order flow — paper-trading uses simulated fills only

Does NOT provide personalized financial advice or act as an RIA/broker-dealer — this is a personal decision-support tool for the founder, not a distributed product with users other than the founder's trusted circle in v1
Is NOT a general public SaaS in v1 — no freemium tiers, no billing, no public signup flow
Does NOT push alerts directly into a user's TradingView account (no such public API exists) — TradingView integration is Pine Script export only
Does NOT guarantee negation timing or pattern outcomes — negation is modeled as probabilistic, not certain, and every recommendation carries a risk score alongside confidence

business.model: Internal decision-support tool for the founder (and, from v1.1, a small trusted circle of co-traders). No subscription/billing in MVP; revisit only if a public product direction is later decided.
compliance: Read-only market-data integration only — no order placement code path exists; this must remain true even as the codebase grows (enforced by code review discipline in CLAUDE.md, not a runtime toggle that could be flipped)
Every recommendation, backtest run, and trade-journal entry is written to an append-only audit log for the founder's own record-keeping and post-mortem analysis
Respect Zerodha Kite Connect's API terms of service (rate limits, data redistribution restrictions) — no redistribution of Zerodha market data outside this tool
No storage of Zerodha trading credentials with order-placement scope; only the read-only Kite Connect API key/access token are configured
SEBI algo-trading rules around automated order placement are a non-issue by construction, since no order-placement path exists
risks: Negation-timing heuristic (MVP) may under-perform a trained model until enough labeled trade-journal data accumulates — mitigated by showing risk score alongside confidence and requiring manual trade execution regardless
Zerodha Kite Connect API changes or rate limits could disrupt live data ingestion — mitigated by Redis caching and graceful degradation (skip a cycle rather than fire a stale-data recommendation)

False confidence from an over-fit heuristic on limited historical data — mitigated by requiring the strategy marketplace's independent backtest step before any strategy (including the founder's own) is trusted
India VIX/expiry-day volatility producing noisy signals — mitigated by the risk-guardrail thresholds and explicit low-conviction/BTST framing on expiry days
Strategy-marketplace video-to-logic extraction may mis-parse ambiguous trading logic — mitigated by requiring an independent backtest pass before a marketplace strategy is surfaced as usable
open_questions: Which news/sentiment vendor (if any) to integrate — none was named in the session; left as a pluggable, disabled-by-default interface (see docs/assumptions.md)
Exact VIX regime thresholds and heavyweight watchlist composition — defaulted sensibly, configurable, listed as assumptions for founder review
When (if ever) to expand beyond the founder's personal use to a small trusted circle, and what auth/access-control implications that has
Whether to eventually pursue a trained LSTM negation model vs. continuing to refine the heuristic, once enough trade-journal data exists

similar_repos.reusable: Candlestick pattern recognition (Engulfing, Harami, Doji, etc.) — wrap rather than reimplement pattern detection math.
Indicator library (RSI, SuperTrend, VWAP, moving averages) computed directly on pandas OHLCV frames pulled from TimescaleDB.
Independent backtest engine for the Strategy Marketplace — NumPy-native, fast enough for repeated backtests across many candidate strategies.
Zerodha Kite Connect API docs — historical candles, live quotes, WebSocket ticks, OI/Greeks. Used strictly read-only in this project.
similar_repos.license: Proprietary API — ToS governs usage, not a code dependency

similar_repos.reusable: Telegram bot for dispatching fired recommendations.
similar_repos.license: LGPL-3.0 (note: used as a library dependency, not modified/redistributed — verify LGPL dynamic-linking compliance before shipping outside personal use)

best_in_class: Sensibull / Opstra — pre-calculated OI heatmaps and options analytics (benchmark for OI visualization, not integrated directly)
TradingView — charting + Pine Script ergonomics (benchmark for the Strategy Marketplace's export format)
QuantConnect — backtesting rigor and strategy lifecycle management (benchmark for the independent backtest engine's trustworthiness)

tech.frontend: React + TypeScript SPA (Vite), Recharts for candle/equity visualizations, a collapsible tree component for the AI reasoning view. Dark-first, data-dense dashboard aesthetic per the session's UI direction.
tech.backend: Python 3.11 + FastAPI. Chosen for async I/O (live tick/WebSocket ingestion), and because the entire pattern/ML ecosystem (pandas, TA-Lib, pandas-ta, vectorbt, PyTorch upgrade path) is Python-native.
tech.database: TimescaleDB (PostgreSQL 15 extension) for OHLCV/OI time-series hypertables with continuous aggregates for multi-timeframe rollups (5m base -> 10m/15m/30m/1h/2h/3h/1d), plus standard relational tables for recommendations, trade journal, strategies, and audit log.
tech.auth: Email/password + JWT, bcrypt-hashed passwords. Single founder account in MVP; schema supports adding trusted co-traders in v1.1 without a migration.
tech.hosting: Docker Compose for local dev and single-VM deploy (services: api, worker, timescaledb, redis, frontend). No Kubernetes in MVP.
tech.market_data: Zerodha Kite Connect (read-only: historical candles, live quotes, WebSocket ticks, OI/Greeks, India VIX, NIFTY50/BANKNIFTY/sector index quotes). No order-placement scope requested or used.
tech.ml: MVP: heuristic/statistical negation-timing model (historical average candles-to-negate per pattern x timeframe x VIX regime) computed by a nightly backtest job. v1.1+: PyTorch LSTM sequence model once labeled trade-journal data is sufficient. Bayesian weight updates for pattern/negation confidence from manual trade-outcome feedback.

llms.task: Rationale-tree narration: convert the deterministic engine's structured factor scores into a readable, layered explanation per recommendation
llms.why: Narrates numbers the engine already computed; never itself decides confidence, risk, entry, or exit, and has no access to order-placement code paths.

llms.task: Strategy Marketplace video/text ingestion: extract canonical trading logic (indicators, thresholds, entry/exit rules) from a submitted video transcript or free-text strategy description
llms.model: Claude (Anthropic), fed a transcript produced by a separate speech-to-text step (e.g. Whisper) for video sources
llms.why: Single LLM vendor for a solo-founder MVP rather than the session's loosely-mentioned 'GPT-4 + Claude' combination; see docs/assumptions.md.

ui.style_direction: Dark-first, data-dense trading-terminal aesthetic. Monospace for prices/indicators. Green/red reserved for bullish/bearish and P&L only. Recommendation cards are scannable at a glance; the reasoning tree is collapsed by default and expands on demand.
key_screens: Dashboard home: forecast-horizon selector (15m/30m/1h/2h), category tabs (Tactical / Impulse / Strategic / BTST), recommendation cards with confidence + risk + conviction

Recommendation deep-dive: collapsible AI reasoning tree (macro bias -> pattern -> negation prediction -> heavyweight confirmation -> OI -> seasonality -> final score) with the underlying data points shown at each layer
Watchlist & risk settings: heavyweight constituent list, sector list, VIX regime thresholds, expiry-day dampening toggle, user-defined strike-selection rules

Strategy Marketplace: ingestion form (video URL / text / pseudocode / Pine Script), strategy list with backtest status + confidence score, fusion (combine two strategies) action, Pine Script / Python export
Paper trading: mode switch (paper vs. live-manual), open simulated positions, simulated P&L and equity curve
Trade journal / feedback: log outcome + observation against a past recommendation, feeding the pattern/negation weight-update job
Alerts settings: Telegram bot linkage, email destination, per-category alert on/off
Login (single founder account, extensible to a trusted circle in v1.1)
user_flows: Open dashboard -> pick a forecast horizon -> scan Tactical recommendation cards -> expand reasoning tree on the top pick -> execute manually in Zerodha's own app -> log the outcome later in the trade journal
Receive a Telegram alert on a fired Impulse-category recommendation -> open dashboard deep-dive -> decide to act or skip

Submit a YouTube strategy video -> Strategy Agent extracts logic -> review extracted pseudocode -> run independent backtest -> if confidence is acceptable, fuse with an existing rule set or keep standalone -> export Pine Script
Toggle Paper mode -> let recommendations auto-fill as simulated trades -> review simulated P&L before trusting a new rule set live-manual
On an expiry day, see tactical recommendations flagged low-conviction with a warning banner, alongside a BTST suggestion if the pattern supports it

acceptance_criteria: Given live Zerodha Kite market data is flowing, when the pattern engine runs on a 5m candle close, then any Engulfing/3-candle/Harami/Doji/Pin-Bar pattern on NIFTY, BANKNIFTY, or a watchlist constituent is detected and written to `patterns_detected` within one polling cycle.

Given a detected pattern, when the negation model evaluates it, then a predicted negation window (candle count + VIX-regime-adjusted time estimate) is written to `negation_predictions` with a model_version tag.

Given a pattern, its negation prediction, heavyweight/sector confirmation, OI data, and RSI, when the confidence engine scores it, then a confidence score (0-100) is computed using the documented weighted formula and a separate risk score and conviction score are produced alongside it.

Given a scored setup, when a recommendation is generated, then it is categorized as exactly one of Tactical/Impulse/Strategic/BTST, includes entry/exit/strike (for Tactical/Impulse/BTST) or direction/accumulation-zone (for Strategic), and includes a rationale tree referencing the actual factor scores used.

Given any recommendation, when a user requests the deep-dive view, then the full reasoning tree renders with every contributing layer and its underlying data points — never a black-box score with no explanation.

Given India VIX is in the Extreme regime (>30 by default, configurable), when the recommendation engine runs, then no new Tactical recommendations are generated and any Strategic/BTST output is clearly flagged low-conviction.
Given an expiry day, when Tactical recommendations are generated, then conviction is dampened and a BTST alternative is surfaced if the pattern supports it.
Given a fired recommendation, when Telegram/dashboard alerts are enabled, then the alert is dispatched and an `alerts_log` row is written with dispatch status.
Given a past recommendation, when the user logs a trade-journal outcome (win/loss + observation), then the entry is persisted and included in the next scheduled pattern/negation weight-update job.

Given Paper mode is active, when a recommendation fires, then a simulated position is opened and tracked to a simulated exit with P&L recorded — no real order is ever placed in either Paper or Live-manual mode.

Given a strategy is submitted to the Marketplace (video URL, text, pseudocode, or Pine Script), when the Strategy Agent processes it, then canonical logic is extracted and an independent backtest against historical TimescaleDB data returns a confidence score before the strategy is marked usable.
Given two strategies are fused, when the fusion engine merges them, then the combined rule set is independently backtested before being surfaced as a usable fused strategy.
Given the full stack, when a developer runs `docker compose up`, then api, worker, timescaledb, redis, and frontend all start and the dashboard is reachable locally.
Given the entire codebase, when it is audited for order-placement code paths, then none exist — this is a structural acceptance criterion, not just a runtime configuration.
build_prompt:
Build the Trading Intelligence Platform (TIP): a personal decision-support tool for NIFTY 50 / BANKNIFTY F&O day trading. It is READ-ONLY on market data and NEVER places, modifies, or cancels a real order anywhere in the codebase — recommendations are notify/manual-execute only. Two execution modes exist: Paper-trade (simulated fills) and Live-manual (system recommends, founder executes manually in Zerodha's own app). Do not add an order-placement path even as a disabled flag.

STACK: Python 3.11 + FastAPI backend, TimescaleDB (PostgreSQL 15) for OHLCV/OI time-series + relational tables, Redis for live-tick cache and rate limiting, React + TypeScript + Vite frontend (Recharts), JWT email/password auth (bcrypt), Docker Compose (services: api, worker, timescaledb, redis, frontend). Zerodha Kite Connect for read-only market data (historical candles, live quotes, WebSocket ticks, OI/Greeks, India VIX). Claude (Anthropic) for rationale-tree narration and Strategy Marketplace logic extraction only — never for numeric decisions.

CORE ENGINE:
1. Multi-timeframe candlestick pattern detection (TA-Lib + pandas-ta: Engulfing, 3-candle inside/outside, Harami, Doji, Pin Bar) across 5m/10m/15m/30m/1h/2h/3h for NIFTY, BANKNIFTY, and a configurable top-15-by-weight heavyweight + sector watchlist.
2. Candle-negation cascade: MVP heuristic model (historical avg candles-to-negate per pattern x timeframe x VIX regime); schema supports a v1.1 LSTM upgrade without migration.
3. Multi-timeframe support/resistance (pivot + hit-frequency confluence, Weekly down to 5m).
4. Heavyweight/sector correlation scoring against the index-level pattern.
5. Intraday seasonality + impulse-move detection at 10:00/11:00/12:30/13:30/14:00/14:30-15:00 IST, as a distinct Impulse recommendation category.
6. Confidence scoring: Confidence = 0.25*macro_SR + 0.25*heavyweight_alignment + 0.20*strike_candle_pattern + 0.15*OI_accumulation + 0.15*RSI_alignment. Separate risk score (VIX regime, expiry proximity, liquidity) and conviction score.
7. Four recommendation categories: Tactical (15m/30m/1h/2h entry/exit/strike), Impulse, Strategic (2-5 day outlook), BTST (expiry-adjacent).
8. Collapsible AI reasoning tree per recommendation, narrated by Claude from the engine's actual factor scores.
9. User-configurable strike-selection rules overriding system defaults per user.
10. Feedback loop: manual trade-journal outcome logging -> Bayesian pattern/negation weight-update job.
11. Paper-trading simulator with simulated fills and P&L; explicit Paper/Live-manual mode switch.
12. Strategy Marketplace: ingest video URL/text/pseudocode/Pine Script -> Claude-based logic extraction -> vectorbt-based independent backtest -> confidence score -> Pine Script/Python export -> fusion engine for combining strategies (independently re-backtested before use). Seed the marketplace with the founder's own 'Breakout VWAP Retracement' (BVWR) rule set.
13. Telegram bot + dashboard + optional email alerts on fired recommendations.
14. Risk guardrails: configurable VIX regime thresholds (default Normal<15, Elevated 15-20, High 20-30, Extreme>30 suppresses new Tactical recs), expiry-day conviction dampening, low-liquidity no-trade flags.

UI: dark-first trading-terminal aesthetic, monospace numerics, green/red for direction/P&L only. Screens: Dashboard (horizon selector + category tabs + recommendation cards), Deep-dive reasoning tree, Watchlist & risk settings, Strategy Marketplace, Paper trading, Trade journal/feedback, Alerts settings, Login.

VERIFY: `docker compose up` starts all five services and the dashboard is reachable; a full codebase audit finds zero order-placement code paths; pattern detection, negation prediction, confidence/risk/conviction scoring, and reasoning-tree rendering all work end-to-end on sample historical data; Paper mode produces simulated fills only; Strategy Marketplace ingestion -> extraction -> independent backtest -> confidence score works for at least the seeded BVWR strategy. Deliver with tests covering every acceptance criterion.
